name: Carrot Soup with Coriander
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel the carrots and cut them into half-inch pieces (about 5-6 cups).
2. Peel the potato and cut into small cubes (about 1 1/2 cups).
3. In a large saucepan over medium-low heat, melt the butter.
4. When hot, add the onion and sauté while stirring occasionally until translucent, about 5-6 minutes.
5. Add the garlic and sauté while stirring until beginning to change color, 20-30 seconds.
6. Add the carrots, potatoes, and coriander and stir a couple of times for about 2-3 minutes.
7. Add the chicken broth, sugar, and salt; bring to a simmer over medium heat, lower the heat, cover partially, and continue simmering until vegetables are soft when pierced with the point of a knife, about 20-25 minutes.
8. Transfer the vegetable mixture to a food processor with its metal blade attached or a blender, process in small batches with their broth until a smooth puree (soup may be made ahead to this point; cover and refrigerate).
9. Return the puree to the saucepan and add the milk and pepper to taste.
10. Place over medium heat and heat almost to a boil. Taste for seasoning and adjust as necessary. If too thick, add more milk.
11. Just before serving, stir in the sherry to your taste.
12. To serve: Ladle into warm soup bowls. Top each bowl with 1-2 spoonfuls of sour cream and a generous sprinkling of chopped cilantro.
